_meta.purpose: Curated overlay linking recorded DPWH awardees to official actions, court filings, and named news coverage. Every entry carries a source link. Firms with no confirmed action carry recorded facts only.
_meta.disclaimer: All data sourced from public records (COA, SEC, DBM, PSA, BSP, SALN disclosures) and named court, agency, and news reports. This tool computes statistical indicators only. Charges are allegations under the presumption of innocence; specific allegations, if any, require independent investigation and corroboration.
_meta.verification_note: Official actions (PCAB revocation, Ombudsman and Sandiganbayan filings, DPWH orders, SEC and Comelec resolutions, AMLC freeze orders, DOJ lookout orders) are cross-checked against a primary or primary-citing news source. Where a matter was resolved in a party's favour (for example the Comelec clearing a campaign-finance complaint), that outcome is recorded alongside the allegation.

investigation.chair: Ret. SC Assoc. Justice Andres Reyes Jr.
investigation.note: Investigated flagged flood-control projects; referred individuals to the Ombudsman and DOJ. Final report held pending release decision.
Subpoenaed the highest-volume contractors; Sarah Discaya testified Sept 1, 2025.
Filed the first criminal charges before the Sandiganbayan (Nov 2025).
Issued fraud audit reports on Bulacan flood-control projects.
Secured court freeze orders; about PHP 13 billion in assets frozen by December 2025.
Issued lookout bulletin orders covering 43 officials and contractors.

context.top15_note: News reporting and the Marcos administration's own review found that about 15 contractors received roughly PHP 100 billion, near 20% of flood-control program spending. Being a high-volume awardee is not an allegation, but a descriptive fact from the records.

persons.name: Cezarah "Sarah" Discaya
persons.role: Admitted owner/officer of nine construction firms (Senate testimony, Sept 1, 2025)
persons.status: Firms' PCAB licenses revoked (Board Resolution 075, Sept 2025). AMLC froze accounts tied to the family (news-reported).

persons.name: Pacifico "Curlee" Discaya
persons.status: Firms' PCAB licenses revoked (Board Resolution 075, Sept 2025).

persons.name: Elizaldy "Zaldy" Co
persons.role: Resigned representative; linked to Sunwest, Inc.
persons.status: Charged by the Ombudsman before the Sandiganbayan (graft, malversation), Nov 2025. Reported to have left the country; the Sandiganbayan archived the case in 2026 while he remained at large. Charges are allegations; case unresolved.

persons.name: Eumir and Pamela Villanueva
persons.role: Owners of Topnotch Catalyst Builders Inc.
persons.status: Named on the DOJ immigration lookout bulletin (Sept 2025). A lookout order is not a charge, but an investigative step.

persons.name: Lawrence R. Lubiano
persons.role: President of Centerways Construction and Development Inc.
persons.status: Reported as the top campaign donor of Senate President Chiz Escudero in 2022. The Comelec cleared both of election-finance liability; Escudero denied any role in the firm's deals. Not an allegation of wrongdoing.

persons.name: Alex H. Abelido
persons.status: Named on the DOJ immigration lookout bulletin (Sept 2025). A lookout order is not a charge, but an investigative step.

persons.name: MG Samidan Construction (Samidan family)
persons.status: Subpoenaed by the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee; submitted documents to the ICI. No charge filed as of mid-2026.

persons.name: Mark Allan Arevalo
persons.status: Firm perpetually disqualified by DPWH for ghost projects (Sept 2025).

The Ombudsman charged company directors and Zaldy Co before the Sandiganbayan (graft under RA 3019 and malversation) over the PHP 289.5-million Naujan, Oriental Mindoro flood-control project, distributed to the 5th, 6th and 7th Divisions (Nov 2025). Charges are allegations; the case is pending.
Co is reported to have left the country; the Sandiganbayan archived the case in 2026 while he remained at large. The matter is unresolved.
DPWH ordered perpetual disqualification for ghost flood-control projects in Bulacan (DPWH Secretary order).

Named in COA Fraud Audit Reports (four reports, about PHP 297 million) filed with the Independent Commission for Infrastructure over DPWH Bulacan 1st District Engineering Office flood-control projects. COA cited ghost projects (no structure at the approved site despite documents marked 100% complete), unauthorized relocation of sites without written authority, and payments for structures that pre-existed the award. One project was a joint venture with SYMS Construction in Baliwag. Audit period July 2022 to May 2025. A COA fraud-audit finding is an allegation referred for investigation, not a court finding.
DPWH ordered perpetual disqualification for ghost flood-control projects in Bulacan (DPWH Secretary order).

A SYMS flood-control structure along the Angat River at Barangay Taal, Pulilan, Bulacan (contract about PHP 92.7 million) was the subject of a COA Fraud Audit Report as a ghost project: COA found no structure at the approved site, DPWH pinpointed a different location without explanation, critical supporting documents were missing, and the firm was paid in full. A fraud-audit finding is an allegation, not a court finding.

Named alongside Wawao Builders in the set of four COA Fraud Audit Reports (about PHP 297 million) over DPWH Bulacan 1st District Engineering Office flood-control projects filed with the Independent Commission for Infrastructure.

Subject of a COA Fraud Audit Report over a Riverbank Protection Structure (Package B) at Barangay Bagong Silang, Plaridel, Bulacan (contract PHP 69,480,116). COA flagged it as a ghost project that was fully paid despite missing critical supporting documents. A fraud-audit finding is an allegation referred for investigation, not a court finding.
Owners named on the DOJ immigration lookout bulletin order (Sept 2025).
Named in news tracking of Ombudsman and ICI referrals arising from the flood-control review; any charge would be an allegation, pending.

Reported as the top campaign donor of Senate President Chiz Escudero in 2022 (about PHP 30 million, near 20% of Escudero's cash contributions). About 96% of the firm's 2021-2024 flood-control projects are in the Bicol region. A campaign donation is not an allegation of wrongdoing by either party, but a matter of public record.
The Comelec cleared both Escudero and Lubiano of election-finance liability; Escudero denied any role in the firm's deals.
The firm's president was named on the DOJ immigration lookout bulletin order (Sept 2025). A lookout order is not a charge, but an investigative step.

President Marcos said initial findings suggested several of the firm's flood-control projects were incomplete or nonexistent; news reporting noted many of its projects were swamped in the July 2025 monsoon.
SEC records show about PHP 250,000 paid-up capital while the firm won roughly PHP 5 billion in flood-control deals, per news reporting.
Subpoenaed by the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee and submitted documents to the ICI. No charge filed as of mid-2026.

The procuring office at the centre of the COA fraud audit on Bulacan flood control. COA filed four Fraud Audit Reports (about PHP 297 million) with the Independent Commission for Infrastructure over projects implemented by this office and awarded to Wawao Builders and SYMS Construction, citing ghost projects with no structure at the approved site, unauthorized relocation of sites, and payments for structures that pre-existed the award. In one report, office representatives led inspectors to a location different from the approved plans without written authority. Audit period July 2022 to May 2025. Findings are allegations referred for investigation, not court findings.
